title: Grading Rubric
description: Schema for a lab grading rubric used by the automated grading pipelines.

GlobalScoring.description: Lab-wide scoring rules applied to all exercises.

Breakdown.description: Labels for the three scoring sub-criteria (e.g. 'CodeExecution (1 pt)').

DiscretionaryPenaltyMax.description: Maximum discretionary deduction that can be applied per exercise.

Rules.description: Ordered list of grading rules the model must follow.

^Ex[0-9]+$.description: Grading criteria for a single exercise (e.g. Ex1, Ex2, ...).

Points.description: Maximum points available for this exercise.

Criteria.description: Short description of what the exercise tests.

Checks.description: Three named sub-criteria, each worth a fixed number of points. Keys follow the pattern '<label> (N pt)'. Point values may vary by lab.

^CodeExecution.description: A detailed definition of what to expect of correct code execution in a correct answer, including the control flow, i.e. the sequence of function calls. Reference all functions used, and all function calls expected. Add a single parens '(' after every function referenced. Enclose all references with back-ticks.

^ProcessFidelity.description: A detailed definition of the process that leads to a correct answer. Describe in detail the sequence and order of steps, along with the number of steps. Name all the variables that should be created and denote that as 'expected'. Enclose all referenced variables with back-ticks.

^OutputAccuracy.description: A detailed description of the output expected in a correct answer. State definitively the numerical value expected or the number and dimensions of the data structures created. If possible note the column names of the tibble or data.frames that should be generated by a correct answer. Enclose all referenced column names with back-ticks.

additionalProperties.description: Discretionary penalty note. Key is expected to match 'DiscretionaryPenalty (up to -N pt)'.
